search_queries_used: site:who.int surgical safety checklist equipment problems sign out
site:asahq.org documentation anesthesia care equipment intraoperative event
site:doh.gov.ae day surgery equipment incident documentation
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic management or dose instructions for Anesthesia equipment issue documentation
generic anesthesia landing pages without an exact section for Anesthesia equipment issue documentation
rejection_reasons: Device troubleshooting and response depend on the exact device, event, facility policy, and qualified staff.
The candidate source category "automatic equipment failure response algorithms" did not provide exact documentation-only evidence for this workflow and was not mapped.
Autonomous management, medication, dose, procedure, escalation, or discharge instructions were outside the documentation-only scope.
Landing pages and search snippets were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Patients undergoing surgery or procedures where an anesthesia or procedural equipment issue was identified.
setting_applicability: Direct for operating-room or procedural Sign Out documentation; biomedical engineering and incident-management processes remain separate.
UAE_applicability: WHO evidence is globally applicable but requires DoH facility policy and local incident-reporting adaptation.
recency_verification: WHO 2009 checklist, current ASA 2023 documentation statement, and DoH V1/2024 standard were reviewed on 2026-07-13; WHO recency is a limitation.
superseded_check: WHO 2009 remains the official checklist reference; current local adaptation is still required.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO Sign Out section supports recording anesthesia or surgical equipment problems before leaving the operating room; it does not diagnose the failure or direct corrective action.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented procedure name, counts, specimen labeling, equipment problems, and key recovery or management concerns without generating a plan.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Anesthesia equipment issue documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

unresolved_source_gaps: Device identity, failure mechanism, patient impact, immediate response, incident report, maintenance action, and regulatory notification remain unsupported unless clinician-entered.
Legacy duration presets, broad generic interval-history wording, and any content not explicitly mapped to an exact section remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No source mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, anesthesia plan, investigation, procedure, medication, dose, referral, discharge decision, or urgency conclusion.
UAE facility policy, credentialing, pathway, and qualified anesthesiologist review remain required before use.
